observations.recommendation:
1.10. We recommended that the Inventory Committee in coordination with the MGSO conduct the Physical Count of all PPEs of the LGU and submit the Report on Physical Count of PPE not later than July 30, 2025.
1.11. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a complete and detailed PPE Ledger Cards, and that the MGSO do the same for Property Cards covering all LGU properties. This will strengthen record-keeping, ensure accountability, and support the accuracy of financial records.
1.12. During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant, the MGSO and the Inventory Committee members who are present agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

2.7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the received Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment under Property, Plant and Equipment and the corresponding Accounts Payable of ₱7,199,998.00 in the books of accounts.
2.8. During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant explained that she was not furnished with documents regarding the delivery of the above-mentioned equipment, hence the non-recording of such in CY 2024. Moreover, she agreed with the recommendation and will record in the books of accounts the above equipment totaling ₱7,199,998.00.

3.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant drop the aforementioned properties totaling ₱786,539.00 from the PPE account to arrive at the correct account balance.
3.9. We also recommended that, moving forward, the Municipal Accountant comply with COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022 and COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024 for the proper treatment of semi-expendable properties.
3.10. During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant agreed with the audit recommendations and will drop in the PPE account the aforementioned properties totaling ₱786,539.00.

4.8. We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components of all road projects totaling ₱442,072,993.46.
4.9. Moreover, we recommended that the OIC Municipal Assessor inspect and assess the cost of the road lot component of all road networks within the territorial jurisdiction of LGU-Abuyog and provide data to the Municipal Accountant, GSO and Municipal Engineer.
4.10. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the Road Lot component of the Municipal Road projects based on the report of the Municipal Assessor, maintain the LRNLC with complete description of each road component, and Disclose in the Notes to FS in the ensuing years the breakdown of the Road Network Account.
4.11. Likewise, we recommended that the GSO prepare and maintain complete and detailed LRNPC for all roads and its components and in the ensuing years, prepare the Report on Local Road Network.
4.12. Finally, we recommended that the Inventory Committee, use the required format provided in COA Circular 2015-008 in reporting the Physical Count of Road Network System.
4.13. During the exit conference, the Municipal Engineer, the Municipal Assessor, the Municipal General Service Officer and the Municipal Accountant agreed with the audit recommendations, and committed to implement them.
5.14. During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

6.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a correcting entry to restore the amount of ₱2,498,500.00 to the Due to NGAs account and prepare and submit the Fund Utilization Reports of the same to the source agency in order to recognize its income.
6.9. Also, we reiterate our previous year’s recommendation that income should be recognized only upon liquidation.
6.10. The Municipal Accountant committed to prepare and submit the FUR of the above-mentioned disbursement amounting ₱2,498,500.00, and agreed to comply with the recommendation that recognition of income should only be made upon liquidation.

7.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Local Development Council, and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, identify and include in the AIP priority projects which are ready for implementation in the current budget year to ensure optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund.
7.10. We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer:
d. Strictly enforce the project durations stipulated in the contracts thru effective monitoring;
e. Diligently inspect completed projects immediately after the reported date of completion to verify timeliness; and
f. Ensure that all materials and procedures comply with the Program of Work, Detailed Estimates and Plans.
7.11. During the exit conference, the Municipal Engineer agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

9.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all insurable LGU properties are covered under the General Insurance Fund (GIF) of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS), in compliance with R.A. No. 656 and the guidelines outlined in COA Circular 2018-002.
9.10. In future application of insurance with the GSIS GIF, we also recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer prepare the PIF and submit the same to GSIS not later than April 30 of each year to ensure sufficiency of insurance coverage and protection in case of unforeseen natural or man-made hazards. Furnish copy of the PIF and proof of submission to GSIS to the COA Audit Team for evaluation of the actions taken.
9.11. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant, trace back previous years transaction and record in the books the cost of the insured Motor Vehicles not found in the accounting records.
9.12. During the exit conference, the GSO and Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the audit recommendations. In addition, the Municipal Budget Officer also stated that moving forward, they will increase their appropriations for insurance.

10.9. We recommended that the GFPS Technical Working Group and GAD Focal Person submit the required CY 2024 GAD AR pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, as amended.
10.10. During the exit conference, the GAD Focal Person explained that the preparation of GAD Accomplishment Report is still on going as they still need to coordinate with the accounting office with the data on actual disbursements. He committed that the report will be submitted to the Audit Team once it is done.

11.14. We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, with the help of the GAD Focal person, systematically gather updated data regarding various GAD information on gender statistics and age and sex- disaggregated data and consequently, update the existing Gender and Development database.
11.15. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order to re-organize the GFPS Executive Committee and TWG and establish the GAD M&E Team, in accordance with the personnel requirements outlined in JMC 2013-01, and clearly define their roles and responsibilities.
11.16. During the exit conference, the audit recommendations are well taken by the GAD Focal Person and he committed that they will comply with the updating of GAD Database.

12.6. We recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO):
a. Reconcile records with GSIS and promptly update employees on any necessary changes to their employment status. These updates should be communicated to the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) using the prescribed GSIS format to ensure timely and accurate record updates; and
b. Ensure that all premium contributions and loan amortizations are deducted from employees' salaries in the payroll for the month they are due.
12.7. We also recommended that the AAO:
a. Promptly update employees' employment status and salary adjustments in the GSIS Web MPS upon receiving the necessary information from the HRMO; and
b. Monitor the approval of employees' loan applications and immediately inform the HRMO and Municipal Accountant to ensure timely deductions from payroll.
12.8. Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. provide the Audit Team with an explanation of the discrepancies identified in the GSIS billing, the amounts withheld from contributions and loan deductions, and the corresponding remittances as reflected in Tables 21 and 22; and
b. conduct monthly reconciliations of employee premium contributions and loan amortizations by comparing GSIS billings with actual payroll deductions and remitted amounts. This practice ensures billing accuracy, facilitates timely remittances to GSIS, and guarantees correct salary deductions.
12.9. During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant explained that the variances noted were due to timing differences and she also assured that she will provide the Audit Team a matrix on every variance noted and provide explanation thereof.

13.9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the accounting records to verify the accuracy of the unaccounted/unsettled "Due to PhilHealth" account, which has a balance of ₱148,391.81 for General Fund and ₱94,391.81 for Trust Fund. Additionally, the unsettled balance per the Statement of Premium Account (SPA) from PhilHealth, totaling ₱434,809.70, should be examined. Any confirmed outstanding amounts must be remitted to PhilHealth without delay.
13.10. We also recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer promptly coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to update employees' employment status and basic monthly salary. This will ensure that PhilHealth records reflect accurate and up-to-date information.
13.11. During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant explained that the variances noted were due to timing differences and she also assured that she will provide the Audit Team a matrix on every variance noted and provide explanation thereof.

14.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Remitting Officer:
a. Exert diligent efforts to account the beginning balance of Due to Pag-IBIG account and make the necessary adjustments and/or correction if necessary;
b. Coordinate with the Pag-IBIG Office to verify whether the LGU has any unpaid contributions. If any are identified, settle the same accordingly; and
c. Ensure the timely and accurate remittance of Pag-IBIG contributions, in compliance with applicable laws and regulations, to avoid interest charges.
14.9. During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

15.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant in coordination with Remitting Officer, immediately compute the correct tax liability for each affected employee and official, and reflect the corresponding over-withheld amount as a tax refund in a payroll adjustment.
15.9. We also recommended that management, thru the Municipal Accountant, allow regular training to relevant staff on current tax laws and BIR pronouncements to ensure ongoing compliance and prevent future instances of over-withholding.
15.10. During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

16.6. We recommended that the Local School Board and the Municipal Accountant diligently comply with the reportorial requirements in the allocation and utilization on Special Education Fund as outlined in the afore-cited regulations.
16.7. During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the recommendation.

17.13. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit justification as to the disbursements made for the provision of food allowance to select offices.
17.14. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from granting allowances, in cash or in kind, without conclusive legal basis therefor.
17.15. The Municipal Mayor is yet to submit his justification. However, during the exit conference, the LGU officials present agreed to comply on non-granting of allowances without conclusive legal basis.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and LGSO submit written
justification for the above noted deficiencies and fast track the reconciliation
of CY 2016 and prior years’ property records.
We also recommended that the LGSO together with the Inventory Committee,
conduct the Physical Count of properties acquired in CY 2023, 2016 and prior
years and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team on or before June 30, 2024.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare PPE Ledger
Cards for all properties acquired in CY 2022 and prior years.
Finally, we recommended that the LGSO prepare Property Cards for
properties acquired in CY 2023, CY 2016 and prior years and reconcile the
same with the General Ledger balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant drop the aforementioned
properties in the PPE account to arrive at a correct account balance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a correcting entry to
revert the amount of ₱28,761,999.00 to the Due to NGAs account. We reiterate
our previous year’s recommendation that income should only be recognized
upon liquidation.

We reiterated our audit recommendation that the Municipal Accountant
prepare Journal Entry Vouchers to record the valid book reconciling items
per Bank Reconciliation Statements on the month they occurred or were
detected in order to present an accurate and reliable cash balance in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary
adjustments for the above erroneous entry and record the unpaid rent of the
tenants of the Public Market Stall using the Operating Lease Receivable
account.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue demand letters to
tenants with long outstanding unpaid rentals.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned fast
track the implementation of the remaining six unimplemented development
projects and continuously adhere to the provisions of the aforementioned
DBM-DOF-DILG JMC, to ensure that maximum attainment of desired socio-
economic development and environmental management outcomes of the
Municipality are achieved and the intended purposes of the 20% Development
Fund are realized.

We recommended that:
a) the BAC Chairman require the contractors of on-going infrastructure projects
to post additional Performance Security to include the coverage period of one
year from completion date;
b) the BAC Secretariat examine the validity period of Performance Security
posted by winning bidders upon signing of the contract, and ensure that the
coverage period will include one year after the completion of the project, and;
c) the Municipal Mayor issue the Certificate of Final Acceptance one year after
the completion of each project.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Chairperson of the
BAC to submit written justification for the evaluation of the Audit Team for
declaring Cafrencillo Arch Design & Builders, Kauie Builders & Construction
Supplies and RAB Builders and Supply and awarding the projects despite
non-disclosure in their eligibility documents, the contracts which were
previously awarded to them.
Further, we recommended that the BAC exercise due diligence in the review
of the eligibility documents of bidders to detect misrepresentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the MDRRM
Council, and the MDRRM Officer:
a.) Implement during the year the remaining 17 PPAs identified in the
LDRRMFIP in order to effectively increase the level of preparedness of
the municipality during natural calamities;
b.) re-appropriate the balances of completed and unnecessary projects to
other PPAs; and
c.) Provide specific timelines and implement the PPAs under the
LDRRMFIP within the period indicated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor with the LDRRM Officer obtain
and pay for the accident insurance of all Accredited Community Disaster
Volunteers (ACDVs) to protect them in case of death or injury while engaged
in volunteerism activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set-up and maintain Special
Accounts in the GF and prepare all the required financial reports for the three
(3) economic enterprises of the Municipality for monitoring of the operating
performance and viability of these LEEs and to serve as a decision-making
tool of the agency. Transactions pertaining to an economic enterprise should
be recorded in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.
We also recommended that the LEE Managers (personnel in-charge) prepare
the required 5-year business plan for each LEE.
Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Finance Committee conduct the
required Semi-Annual Financial Self-Sufficiency Review and submit and
present the same to the Municipal Mayor and local sanggunian.

We recommend that the MENRO submit justification as to the noted
discrepancies in the supporting documents.

We recommended that Inventory Committee should give priority to the
conduct of complete Inventory Count of PPEs every end of the year and
prepare the Report containing the result of the count of all property of the
LGU and submit to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of the following
year.

For the inventory count as of December 31, 2021, the printed and signed copy
should be submitted to the Audit Team, immediately.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) with the assistance of the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Engineer
verify the records of all the lands and buildings/structures owned by the
LGU which were not included in the RPCPPE.
b) reconcile with the RPCPEE the Ledger Cards of all property of the LGU
including those that were acquired in CY 2015 and prior years.
Adjustments/corrections shall be made in the books and/or in the property
records to put the accounts in order.

Further, we recommended that the General Services Officer validate the
existence of all lands owned by the LGU and see to it that these are free from
illegal settlers.

Finally, we recommended that the Inventory Team prepare property tags
which shall contain the information enumerated in COA Circular 2020-006,
Section 5.7, and attach the same to the PPEs counted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant stop recording assets and
payables unless there is an actual delivery and supported with necessary
documents to prove valid claims of supplier.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize in the books
the issuance of construction materials for distribution amounting 
933,460.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a correcting entry
to set the accounts in order. Henceforth, record assets and payables only if
supported with complete documents to prove that goods are actually delivered
or services had been rendered.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
4.10.1 order the withholding of the payment of any money due to the
employees with unliquidated cash advances, considering that the
Municipal Accountant had already sent for demand to liquidate
their outstanding cash advances last September 24, 2020.
4.10.2 File a request for write off with the Commission on Audit of
unliquidated cash advances of deceased employees based on the list of
the Municipal Accountant duly supported with Death Certificate.

We also recommended that, henceforth, avoid granting cash advance to
employees who have unliquidated cash advance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the uncollected stall
rentals as of December 31, 2021.

We also recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer furnish the
Municipal Accountant with the monthly Billing Statements of stall lessees so
that the pertinent Receivables and Income will be recorded in the books of the
LGU.

We recommended that the OIC- Municipal Treasurer:
6.10.1 submit to the Audit Team an explanation why only Collection Receipt
was attached to the DV;
6.10.2 demand from the payees of the transactions mentioned in paragraph
1.7, a Charge Invoice showing the complete details of the transactions
as evidence of payment and submit to the Audit Team for verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the appropriate
Inventory account all the supplies and materials received from the suppliers
and recognize the related expenses only upon distribution to end users, as
evidenced by the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and pre-
numbered Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) and Distribution Lists from the
end-users for the inventories held for distribution.

We recommended that the Local Development Council re-appropriate the
balances of completed projects to other PPAs so that funds are utilized to its
optimum to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and
environmental outcomes of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare journal entry
voucher to recognize the understatement of the affected accounts. Moving
forward, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant stop the current
practice of recognizing depreciation only upon payment and start computing
depreciation after the projects have been completed.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer transfer the 
8.4M
unexpended DRRMF from the Trust Fund - Main bank account to the Special
Trust Fund - DRRM bank account.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary
correcting entry for the erroneous entries made in the transfer of the
unexpended LDRRMF to the Trust Fund Books.

We recommend that the BAC Chairman and all BAC Members submit
explanation for the Audit Teams evaluation why the winning contractor was
already identified in the Resolutions which was prepared before the bid
opening was made, and why LCRB was already identified ahead of the opening
and evaluation of the bids submitted by the bidders.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Chairperson of the
BAC to submit written justification for the evaluation of the Audit Team for
declaring Cafrencillo Arch Design & Builders and JUST IN Enterprises and
awarding the projects despite non-disclosure in their eligibility documents the
contracts which were previously awarded to them.

Further, we recommended that the BAC exercise due diligence in the review
of the eligibility documents of bidders to detect misrepresentation.

We recommended that the Chairperson of the BAC submit written
explanation for the evaluation of the Audit Team as to the inconsistencies and
alteration of dates in the Performance Bonds and SB Resolutions as against
the dates of the Notices of Award.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
14.8.1 Require the contractors of the completed projects to issue another
Warranty Security to rectify the error in the validity of the warranty
security.
14.8.2 For future similar transactions, sign and issue the Certificate of
Acceptance only after the one-year defects liability period has elapsed
(one year from project completion), and thereafter require the
contractor to post Warranty Security valid for one year from acceptance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer
to submit pictures of tarpaulins for all projects implemented in CY 2021. The
said image should show the tarpaulin actually installed in the location of the
projects. Failure to submit the same will result to the disallowance of the
amount paid for the tarpaulin, as stated in the Program of Work.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Engineer
ensure that tarpaulins containing all necessary information regarding the
projects being undertaken by the Municipality be prepared and installed in
the location of the projects. Also, ensure that a list of all on-going government
PPAs and those for implementation during the year are submitted to COA at
the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the officials
of LGU-Abuyog who are responsible in the accounting and reporting of the
Local Road Network of the LGU to accomplish the procedures outlined in
COA Circular No. 2015-008.
Specifically, we recommended that:
1.12.1 The Municipal Assessor inspect and assess the cost of the road lot
component of all road networks within the territorial jurisdiction of
LGU-Abuyog.
1.12.2 The Municipal Engineer:
a) Continue to inspect and appraise the road networks of the LGU,
together with the Municipal Assessor and segregate each
component;
b) Prepare a complete Report on the Physical Count of Local Road
Network (RPCLRN) with the detailed description and cost of
each component; and
c) Submit the RPCLRN to COA every year-end, copy furnished the
Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer.
1.12.3 The Municipal Accountant:
a) Record the Lot component of each Municipal Road based on the
report of the Municipal Assessor;
b) Maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Cards with complete
data/description of each component of the Road Network; and
c) Disclose in the Notes to FS in the ensuing years the breakdown of
the Road Network account.
1.12.4 The General Services Officer prepare and maintain complete
Property Cards on Road Networks and its components and for every
future procurement of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Disbursement
Voucher and other necessary supporting documents to transfer from the
General Fund to the Special Trust Fund, using the correct accounts, the total
unused CY 2019 QRF and MF-MOOE and to make such transfers in the
succeeding years prior to the closing of the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry
Vouchers to record all valid book reconciling items per Bank Reconciliation
Statements and revert the amount of stale checks in order to present an
accurate cash balance in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the focal persons of
each program/project funded by the National Government to prepare and
submit an Assessment Report on the capability of the Municipality to
undertake and complete in CY 2020, the programs/projects funded in prior
years. Return the funds for programs and projects which the Municipality
can no longer undertake.
We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Engineer and Program
Coordinator accelerate the completion of projects/programs which are
identified as viable in order to provide the beneficiaries the benefits that are
expected therefrom.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer return immediately all unutilized balances of all completed
projects to the source agencies to clear the Municipality of its liabilities to the
NGAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Budget Officer stop
using the 20% Development Fund for projects which are for administrative
purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
(c) constitute and activate the Local Finance Committee which will update
the rates of taxes and fees provided in the Tax Ordinance to maximize the
income-generation capabilities of the LGU; and
(d) recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan for approval any proposed
revisions on the Tax Ordinance and implement the same.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
(c) revise and update the format of the Contract of Lease to indicate the
amount of rental based on the newly approved rates, the effectivity date
and period of applicability of the Contract to avoid conflict/ ambiguity; and
(d) require each stall holders to sign a Contract of Lease upon application as
basis for the collection of rentals.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor pay the mandated daily
minimum wage of Job Order workers hired by the LGU as prescribed by the
RTWPB for Region VIII, otherwise, limit the hiring of JOs only to the most
necessary and only to complement the existing workforce of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairman of the Municipal
Solid Waste Management Board, incorporate in the SWM Plan the provision for
attendance by a qualified LGU personnel in necessary trainings, preferably
those conducted by TESDA, who shall cascade his learnings among other
personnel involved in the collection of solid wastes.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator, with the assistance of the GAD Focal Person, gather regularly
various GAD information on gender statistics, age and sex-disaggregated
data and other information to update the existing Gender and Development
database.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person:
10.10.1. ensure submission of the Municipalitys GAD Plan and Budget to
the Leyte Provincial Planning and Development Office to ensure
Municipalitys GAD PPAs alignment to province;
10.10.2. follow up the DILGs approved GAD Plan and Budget, for
incorporation in their annual budgets to be enacted by the Local
Sanggunian;
10.10.3. submit to the Audit Team the copy of the approved GAD Plan and
Budget within five (5) working days upon receipt of its approval by the
DILG; and
10.10.4. fast track the preparation of GAD Accomplishment Report for CY
2019 and every year thereafter and submit to the Audit Team
strictly within five (5) working days after the end of every January.

1.9    We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Inventory
      Committee to complete the physical count of  LGU  properties,  conduct
      the same annually and prepare the  accurate  RPCPPE  to  validate  the
      amounts reported in the books.

1.10  We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the General  Service
      Officer reconcile their respective records, verify  any  discrepancies
      noted and prepare the necessary adjustments to  present  the  accurate
      PPE account balances.

2.10  We recommended that the  Municipal  Engineer  provide  the  Accountant
      with the complete description and cost segregation of road  components
      for  road  projects  as  well  as  the  other  Infrastructure  Assets,
      including the estimated useful life of such assets.

2.11  We also recommended that  the  Municipal  Accountant  prepare  lapsing
      schedules for each infrastructure projects to  determine  the  correct
      Accumulated Depreciation as of  December  31,  2018  and  prepare  the
      necessary correcting entries.

3.6         We recommended that  the  Municipal  Engineer:  (1)  conduct  an
      inventory of all road  projects  of  the  LGU  and  prepare  a  report
      thereon, including  the complete description and cost  segregation  of
      road components for each road project, and (2) provide  the  Municipal
      Accountant and the Head  of  the  General  Services  Office  with  the
      Inventory Report.

3.7          Also,  we  recommended  that  the  Municipal  General  Services
      Officer prepare and maintain complete Local Road Network Property Card
      for all the municipal roads and the Road Map of the LGU.

3.8         Lastly, we recommended that the  Municipal  Accountant  to  keep
      and maintain subsidiary records for  roads  and  road  components  for
      every road network to support the balance of the Road Network account.

4.6    We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as  Chairman  of  the  LDRRM
      Council, identify projects to be funded from the prior  years’  LDRRMF
      in the  Special  Trust  Fund,  incorporate  the same  in  the  LDRRMF
      Investment Plan and implement the project to fully equip the  LGU  and
      its residents in times of disasters.

5.8    We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Engineer  conduct   the   pre-
      engineering survey of each project to ensure its viability.

5.9   We also recommended that the Local  Development  Council  through  the
      Municipal Planning and Development Officer include in the AIP projects
      which are ready for implementation during the budget year in order  to
      utilize fully the 20% Development Fund and provide socio-economic  and
      environmental progress to LGU residents.

6.11  We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:


     1) Issue a  Demand  Letter  to  all  Heads  of  Schools  who  received
        subsidies from the LGU, require the submission of Fund  Utilization
        Reports with the complete supporting documents to  prove  that  the
        funds were used for the intended purpose;


     2) Require recipients of subsidies to sign a Memorandum  of  Agreement
        which should state the purpose of the subsidy, the timeframe of its
        utilization and the  responsibilities  of  both  the  LGU  and  the
        recipients specifically on the submission of FUR once  the  purpose
        have been fulfilled.


6.12  We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:


     3) Defer the grant of additional subsidies unless the  previous  grant
        has been liquidated;
     4) Reclassify the amount  of  ₱200,000.00  as  cash  advances  of  the
        officials who received the amount, and;
     5) Issue Demand Letters to the officials concerned for  its  immediate
        liquidation.

7.10  We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor,  through  Human  Resource
      Management Officer:

     1. Fill up the vacant positions in the  LGU  plantilla  in  accordance
        with CSC rules and regulations;
     2. Reduce the number of Job Order Workers so  that  functions  of  the
        existing regular employees are not duplicated by JOs; and
     3. Pay the mandated daily minimum wage prescribed by RTWPB for  Region
        VIII.

8.6   We recommended that the Municipal  Treasurer:  (1)  issue  immediately
      the checks  for  each  barangay  at  an  amount  equivalent  to  their
      respective RPT shares for CYs 2017 and 2018, and;  (2)  remit,  within
      five (5) days after the end of each quarter, the  amount  due  to  the
      barangays from RPT collected every year.

9.8    We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  reconcile  monthly
      contributions withheld from employees’  salaries  against  the  amount
      remitted to the Philippine Health  Insurance  Corporation  within  the
      prescribed due date.

10.6  We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Payroll  preparer
      obtain the Pag-IBIG Billing Statements  from  all  LGU  employees  who
      opted salary deductions for their loan payments and use it as basis of
      the deductions for loan payments.

11.12  We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (1) compute  the  taxes
      due to the National Government from the operations  of  Abuyog  Hotel;
      (2) file the appropriate returns and pay the  taxes  due  immediately,
      and; (3) file the returns and pay the  taxes  due  in  the  succeeding
      periods. Any penalties and surcharges should be paid by the  employees
      liable for the late payment.

13.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through  the  Municipal  Solid
      Waste  Management  Board  and  the  SWM  Focal  Person,   specifically
      undertake the following:


     a) Allocate funds for  the  development  of  an  operational  Sanitary
        Landfill;
     b) Implement feasible programs that supports  re-use,  recycling,  and
        composting activities;
     c) Assist the 63 barangays in the establishment of their  own  MRF  by
        ensuring that funds are appropriated for such purpose  in  the   CY
        2020 barangay budget;
     d) Require all barangays to segregate solid wastes and monitor closely
        the barangay’s compliance of its implementation;
     e) Disseminate information campaign to adopt measures toward effective
        solid waste management at the barangay level.

We recommended that the (1) Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to complete the physical inventory-taking, and (2) conduct the same annually, in order to prepare a reliable RPCPPE and present the accurate PPE account balances. Also (3) require the Municipal Accountant and General Service Officer to reconcile the balances of their respective property records immediately.
observations.managementAction: (1) Partially Implemented (2) Partially Implemented (3) Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (4) Municipal Mayor provide the necessary assistance to the Municipal Treasurer, in terms of manpower, in order to maximize collection of Real Property/Special Education Taxes through an intensified Tax Collection Campaign program. Likewise, we recommended that the (5) Municipal Treasurer continue its massive drive to collect RPT/SET especially the delinquent accounts by posting the certified list at the main entrance of the Municipal Hall and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each Barangay of the Local Government Unit of Abuyog; We recommended that the (6) Municipal Assessor complete the Tax Mapping in the Municipality to minimize the duplication of records. We further recommended that the (7) Municipal Accountant review the composition of the balances and determine if there are accounts which are impossible to collect or can no longer be traced and consider the same for write-off.
observations.managementAction: (4) Partially Implemented (5) Partially Implemented (6) Not Implemented (7) Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the (8) Municipal Development Council through the Mayor as Chairman, exclude in the Annual Investment Plan activities which are not capital expenditures, otherwise the disbursements shall be disallowed in audit. We also recommended that the (9) Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant appropriate and utilize the 20% Development Fund only for projects identified in the AIP which are capital in nature.

We recommended that the (10) Municipal Accountant review and reconcile monthly taxes withheld to ensure that the entire amount is remitted to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) within the prescribed due date.

We recommended that the (11) Municipal Accountant remit the amount deducted from the employees’ salaries for premium contributions which were not yet remitted totaling P53,767.56. Also, (12) ensure that the actual deductions in the payroll are the exact amount remitted. We also recommended that the (13) Human Resource and Management Officer reconcile the employees’ records with the GSIS and (14) communicate immediately to the said agency any necessary change in the employees’ employment status to update the records of the GSIS and tally with the LGU records.
observations.managementAction: (11) Partially Implemented (12) Partially Implemented (13) Partially Implemented (14) Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (15) Municipal Mayor mandate the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly observe the procurement processes as outlined in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) RA No. 9184 and (16) see to it that the required data/information in the bidding/contract documents are complete.
We recommended that the (17) Local School Board appropriate in the SEF Budget an amount for Early Childhood Care Development for programs and activities which will benefit children aged 0 to 4 years.

We recommended that the (18) Municipal Mayor, as the Chairman of the MCPC, initiate the preparation of the MCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan prioritized according to the needs for integration in the local development plan and (19) ensure that the total funds appropriated for programs, projects and activities to be undertaken by the different departments of the LGU which are attributed to MCPC is equivalent to one percent (1%) of the IRA of the current year.

We recommended that the (20) Municipal Mayor require the official concerned to use prescribed format of the Contract Agreement in the Phil Bidding Documents for all types of procurement to include a stipulation therein the provision for Liquidated Damages so that erring contractors can be held liable in case of non-implementation or delay in the completion of the project.

We recommended that the (21) Municipal Mayor require each program and project coordinator to fast-track the implementation of programs and projects funded by the National Government in accordance with the timeline provided for in the MOA and (22) submit the Liquidation Reports and other documents to the Source Agency immediately.

We recommended that the (23) Municipal Mayor enforce the provisions of the MOA and (24) require recipient barangays to submit Fund Utilization Report together with the necessary attachments as soon as the intended program/project is implemented and (25) defer the grant of additional financial assistance unless the previous financial assistance have been liquidated.
observations.managementAction: (23) Fully Implemented (24) Partially Implemented (25) Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (26) Municipal Mayor collaborate with the barangay officials in the establishment of their own Materials Recovery Facility (MRF). Also, we recommended that the (27) Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, through the Focal Person to construct Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) and (28) sustain the effective implementation of the LGU’s Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.managementAction: (26) Fully Implemented (27) Partially Implemented (28) Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: This is a reiterated finding, thus we repeatedly recommended strict compliance with provisions of the law and rules governing cash advances. We reiterate compliance with the hereunder course of actions, to wit: (29) a. Demand letter shall be made by the Municipal Accountant for the immediate liquidation and settlement of all cash advances outstanding as of December 31, 2015 from concerned accountable officers and employees including the suspension of salaries. Resort to legal action if evidences warrant shall also be made; (30) b. Strict adherence to relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as well as other related pertinent rules and regulations; and (31) c. Immediate submission to the audit team for audit of all original required documents on the settlement and liquidations of all accountable officers.
observations.managementAction: (29) Fully Implemented (30) Fully Implemented (31) Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (32) Local Chief Executive (LCE) should ensure that all concerned officials and employees of the municipality to faithfully discharge their duties and functions for the submission of paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls, collections receipts, deposits and its supporting documents within the reglementary period pursuant to Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 6 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommended that (33) management reclassify the unserviceable properties to their proper asset account in accordance with Section 4 (p) of the Manual of the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Unit Volume I. We also recommended the following measures, to wit: (34) a. Direct the General Services Office (GSO) to prepare and submit the updated list of unserviceable properties containing data on the following a) kind of property; b) description; c) date of acquisition; d) acquisition cost; e) condition; f) accumulated depreciation; g) book value; and h) recommended action; and (35) constitute the appropriate committee or body to undertake the disposal of these assets.
observations.managementAction: (33) Not Implemented (34) Not Implemented (35) Not Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee
to complete the physical inventory-taking, and conduct the same annually, in order
to prepare a reliable RPCPPE and present the accurate PPE account balances.
Also require the Municipal Accountant and General Service Officer to
reconcile the balances of their respective property records immediately.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide the necessary
assistance to the Municipal Treasurer, in terms of manpower, in order to maximize
collection of Real Property/Special Education Taxes through an intensified Tax
Collection Campaign program.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer continue its
massive drive to collect RPT/SET especially the delinquent accounts by posting the
certified list at the main entrance of the Municipal Hall and in a publicly accessible
and conspicuous place in each Barangay of the Local Government Unit of Abuyog;
We recommended that the Municipal Assessor complete the Tax Mapping in
the Municipality to minimize the duplication of records.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the
composition of the balances and determine if there are accounts which are
impossible to collect or can no longer be traced and consider the same for write-off.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Development Council
through the Mayor as Chairman, exclude in the Annual Investment Plan activities
which are not capital expenditures, otherwise the disbursements shall be disallowed
in audit.
We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
Accountant appropriate and utilize the 20% Development Fund only for projects
identified in the AIP which are capital in nature.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and reconcile
monthly taxes withheld to ensure that the entire amount is remitted to the Bureau of
Internal Revenue (BIR) within the prescribed due date.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant remit the amount deducted
from the employees’ salaries for premium contributions which were not yet
remitted totaling P53,767.56. Also, ensure that the actual deductions in the payroll
are the exact amount remitted.
We also recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer
reconcile the employees’ records with the GSIS and communicate immediately to
the said agency any necessary change in the employees’ employment status to
update the records of the GSIS and tally with the LGU records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor mandate the Bids and Awards
Committee to strictly observe the procurement processes as outlined in the Revised
Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) RA No. 9184 and see to it that the
required data/information in the bidding/contract documents are complete .

We recommended that the Local School Board appropriate in the SEF
Budget an amount for Early Childhood Care Development for programs and
activities which will benefit children aged 0 to 4 years.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairman of the MCPC,
initiate the preparation of the MCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan prioritized
according to the needs for integration in the local development plan and ensure that
the total funds appropriated for programs, projects and activities to be undertaken
by the different departments of the LGU which are attributed to MCPC is
equivalent to one percent (1%) of the IRA of the current year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the official concerned to
use prescribed format of the Contract Agreement in the Phil Bidding Documents for
all types of procurement to include a stipulation therein the provision for
Liquidated Damages so that erring contractors can be held liable in case of non-
implementation or delay in the completion of the project.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor: (1) constitute an Inventory Committee who will conduct the annual physical inventory of all PPEs of the LGU; (2) require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory yearly; (3) require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain Property Cards (PCs) of all PPEs to include data on the quantity, condition, date of acquisition and acquisition cost, and; (4) require the Municipal Accountant to update the Property Ledger Cards and have it reconciled with the PCs of the General Services Office.
observations.reason: This is reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require each program and project coordinator to fast-track the implementation of the projects funded by the National Government in accordance with the timeline provided for in the MOA and submit the Liquidation Reports and other documents immediately.
observations.reason: Almost all of the projects are now implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor enforce the provisions of the MOA and require the recipient barangays to submit Fund Utilization Report together with the necessary attachments as soon as the intended program/project is implemented and defer the grant of additional financial assistance unless the previous financial assistance have been liquidated.
observations.reason: Demand Letters were already sent by the accountant to the barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor utilize the 20% Development Fund only for the implementation of environmental, socio-economic development projects to avoid disallowances in audit.
observations.reason: This is reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Disbursement Voucher and the Municipal Treasurer prepare the check for the transfer of the CY 2016 unutilized amounting to P3,780,534.75to the bank account in the Trust Fund maintained for MDRRMF. Also, the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) prepare the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP).

We recommend that the Local School Board of Abuyog, Leyte through the Municipal Mayor as Chairman, prepare the SEF budget prior to the start of the year in order to implement the planned programs and projects within the year and ensure that the desired benefits therefrom are availed by the students.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor collaborate with the barangay officials in the establishment of their own Materials Recovery Facility (MRF). Also, we recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, through the Focal Person to construct Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) and sustain the effective implementation of the LGU’s Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommend strict compliance with the provisions of the law and rules governing cash advances. We reiterate compliance with the hereunder course of actions, to wit: a. demand letter shall be made by the Municipal Accountant for the immediate liquidation and settlement of all cash advances outstanding as of December 31, 2015 from concerned accountable officers and employees including the suspension of salaries. Resort to legal action if evidences warrant shall also be made; b. Strict adherence to relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as well as other related pertinent rules and regulations; and c. Immediate submission to the audit team for audit of all original required documents on the settlement and liquidations of all accountable officers.
observations.reason: Demand Letters were already sent. Previously detailed National Government Agency employees cannot be located but Demand Letters were already sent.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should make necessary adjustments in the books of the LGU and to revert payables to unappropriated surplus which had been outstanding for two years or more and against no actual claimant has been filed. After exhaustion of action towards the establishment of valid claimants to the Payable Accounts. If no such documents are found, reversion of Payable Accounts back to the pertinent equity accounts shall follow. For the matter to be validly acted upon, the provision of COA Circular No. 97-0014 dated February 5, 1997 shall be strictly followed relative to the proper disposition/closure of dormant funds and/or accounts.
We recommend that the utilization of the proceeds of the Special Education Fund be limited to the prioritized programs and activities mandated in Section 272 and 100 (c) of RA 7160.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 in controlling the use of government vehicles, to establish transparency, validity and propriety of the expenditures, and specifically instruct the concerned officers and employees of the agency to comply with the deficiencies noted on the stated transactions to avoid being suspended and disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: Management strictly instructed the concerned officers and employees to comply with the noted deficiencies and avoid suspension and disallowance in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) should ensure that all concerned officials and employees faithfully discharge their duties and functions for the preparation and submission of paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls, collection receipts, deposits and its supporting documents within the reglementary period pursuant to Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 6 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.
observations.reason: Delayed submission for the accounts of January to March is due to the closing of books and preparation of FS.

observations.recommendation: We also recommend management of the LGU to undertake the following measures: a.Direct the General Services Office (GSO) to prepare and submit an updated list of unserviceable properties containing data on the following a) kind of property; b) description; c) date of acquisition; d) acquisition cost; e) condition; f) accumulated depreciation; g) book value; and h) recommended action; and Constitute the appropriate committee or body to undertake the disposal of these assets.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant the immediate submission to COA of supporting documents to prove the existence of claimants to the Payable Accounts. After exhaustion of action towards the establishment of valid claimants to the Payable Accounts and no such documents are found, reversion of Payable Accounts back to the pertinent equity accounts shall follow. For the matter to be validly acted upon, the provision of COA Circular No. 97-0014 dated February 5, 1997 shall be strictly followed relative to the proper disposition/closure of dormant funds and/or accounts.

We recommend that GAD be strictly provided with the full 5% budgetary allocations for it to be fully implemented during the succeeding year’s operations and contribute to the nationwide intent of addressing gender issues and concerns.
We recommend that the management should submit the duly executed contracts and observe the provisions of COA Circular 2009-01 dated February 12, 2009.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor: (1) constitute an Inventory Committee who will conduct the annual physical inventory of all PPEs of the LGU; (2) require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory yearly; (3) require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain Property Cards (PCs) of all PPEs to include data on the quantity, condition, date of acquisition and acquisition cost, and; (4) require the Municipal Accountant to update the Property Ledger Cards and have it reconciled with the PCs of the General Services Office.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require each program and project coordinator to fast-track the implementation of programs and projects funded by the National Government in accordance with the timeline provided for in the MOA and submit the Liquidation Reports and other documents to the Source Agency immediately.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor enforce the provisions of the MOA and require recipient barangays to submit Fund Utilization Report together with the necessary attachments as soon as the intended program/project is implemented anddefer the grant of additional financial assistance unless the previous financial assistance have been liquidated.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor utilize the 20% Development Fund only for the implementation of environmental, socio-economic development projects to avoid disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Disbursement Voucher and the Municipal Treasurer prepare the check for the transfer of the CY 2016 unutilized MDRRMF amounting to P3,780,534.75to the bank account in the Trust Fund maintained for MDRRMF. Also, the Municipal Accountant record the deposit in the Trust Liabilities-DRRM account for the sole purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRM Council within the next five years.

We also recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) prepare the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP).

We recommend that the Municipal School Board of Abuyog, Leyte through the Municipal Mayor as Chairman, prepare the SEF budget prior to the start of the year in order to implement the planned programs and projects within the year and ensure that the desired benefits therefrom are availed by the students.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor collaborate with the barangay officials in the establishment of their own Materials Recovery Facility (MRF). Also, we recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, through the Focal Person to construct Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) and sustain the effective implementation of the LGU’s Solid Waste Management Plan.
